title: Perihelion v1.4.2
content:
Perihelion moved into a more durable image-browser shape this pass. The focus was not on new routing or extra screens, but on making heavy folders behave more predictably by switching the gallery over to cached live thumbnails, restoring a clear large-image rule, and making failed previews degrade gracefully instead of silently disappearing.

What changed:

  • Live cached thumbnails: gallery cards, folder previews, and staging views now request on-demand thumbnails from the backend instead of loading full originals into every preview slot. Generated thumbnails are cached on disk and reused until their source image changes.
  • Large-image rule restored: the Lrg marker is now determined explicitly from the source file, using a 4096px max-dimension threshold so oversized originals stay identifiable even when the gallery shows smaller thumbnails.
  • Preview fallback and retry: if a thumbnail fails, Peri now retries the original image once and then shows a visible fallback card with a retry button instead of leaving a blank tile behind.
  • Folder previews improved: folder covers now prefer the first image file by default, avoiding cases where a PDF or JSON file becomes the preview just because it sorted first.
  • Backend polish: thumbnail responses now use stronger cache semantics, image-dimension checks are memoized more efficiently, and the Python service is prepared for a cleaner shutdown message when stopped intentionally.

This version keeps the same browsing model and visual layout, including the existing 150/200/250/300/400 image-height controls, while making the underlying image delivery much better suited to large archives.
